item.title: Brightline Fields Bankruptcy-Loan Bids While Hoping for a Suitor
item.description: Brightline is weighing bankruptcy-loan offers from its largest creditors after the Florida passenger railroad failed to lure a buyer by a self-imposed bidding deadline last month, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Remains Thin Amid Peace-Deal Uncertainty
item.description: Commercial vessel traffic through the crucial Strait of Hormuz appeared to remain limited over the past day, amid uncertainty over prospects for a US-Iran peace deal.

item.title: Meta’s Rosen, Former Head of Election Integrity, to Depart
item.description: Guy Rosen, Meta Platforms Inc.’s chief information security officer who previously oversaw the company’s election integrity work, is departing in the coming months.

item.title: Denmark’s New Government Plans to Cut Corporate, Income Taxes
item.description: Denmark’s new center-left government said it will cut the corporate tax rate to shore up the Nordic country’s ability to compete in a tougher global environment marked by trade wars and soaring energy costs.

item.title: Vitol Says Europe and US Aren’t Facing Up to Oil Supply Crunch
item.description: Vitol’s top executive in the Middle East said that many Western governments still aren’t reckoning with the oil supply crunch that’s rippling around the world due to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Nestlé CEO Says Lower Coffee, Cocoa Costs Will Boost Margins
item.description: Nestlé SA Chief Executive Officer Philipp Navratil said lower coffee and cocoa prices should help improve margins this year as the foodmaker works to reignite growth.

item.title: Blue Origin’s Lengthy Launchpad Repair Extends SpaceX Dominance
item.description: Blue Origin faces a formidable challenge that goes beyond simply replacing the New Glenn rocket it lost in last week’s explosion: rebuilding a destroyed launchpad that took years to construct.

item.title: Carney Says Data Will Be ‘Uneven’ as Canada Dips Into Technical Recession
item.description: Canadian economic data will be volatile as the government works on a broader transformation of the economy, Prime Minister Mark Carney said in response to last week’s gross domestic product report that showed the country tipped into a technical recession.

item.title: Large Prepaid Energy Deals Poised to Hit Booming Muni Sector
item.description: The muni market is slated to see a prepaid electricity deal amounting to as much as $1.25 billion that will add to the onslaught of supply in the surging market segment.

item.title: Poland’s Leader Calls for Tech Sovereignty to Counter AI Risks
item.description: Poland needs to put technology sovereignty at the top of its political agenda to curb artificial intelligence-related risks threatening its security and economy, Prime Minister Donald Tusk said.

item.title: Triton Is Said to Near €3 Billion Deal for Carlyle’s Flender
item.description: Triton Partners is nearing a deal to buy Flender GmbH, a provider of mechanical and electrical drive technology, in a transaction valuing the firm at about €3 billion ($3.5 billion), according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Job Openings Jump to Highest Since 2024 as Layoffs Fall
item.description: US job openings jumped in April to the highest level in almost two years and layoffs fell, adding to signs the labor market remained resilient even as businesses navigated rising energy costs sparked by the Iran war.

item.title: US Natural Gas Futures Fall on Lower Flows to LNG Export Plants
item.description: US natural gas futures declined as domestic gas flows to liquefied natural gas export terminals along the US Gulf Coast dropped for a third consecutive day. The reduction is concentrated at Sabine Pass LNG in Louisiana and Corpus Christi LNG in Texas.

item.title: Venezuela Hires Hogan Lovells as Counsel for Debt Rework
item.description: Venezuela has retained Hogan Lovells US LLP as legal counsel for what is expected to be one of the largest sovereign debt restructurings in decades.

item.title: Goldman Sees August US Diesel Crunch as Stocks Lowest Since 2003
item.description: Diesel stocks in the US risk reaching a critical threshold of 20 days of supply by August if commercial inventories keep declining at the recent pace amid the near-complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s co-head of global commodities research.

item.title: Legoland Owner’s Junk Bonds Slide as Refinancing Pressure Builds
item.description: Merlin Entertainments Ltd could use some magic. Pressure on family budgets is squeezing the global theme park operator, weighing on its debt as it seeks to refinance junk bonds worth more than $800 million.

item.title: Poland Holds Interest Rates as Inflationary Pressures Cool
item.description: Poland’s central bank kept borrowing costs unchanged for a third month after an unexpected drop in the inflation rate reduced pressure to tighten policy.

item.title: Israel Defense Exports Set Fresh Record for Fifth Straight Year
item.description: Israel’s defense exports hit a record for a fifth consecutive year, with strong demand from Europe and the Asia-Pacific region driving sales.

item.title: Senegal Bonds Go From Emerging-Market Leaders to Laggards as Default Fears Rise
item.description: Senegal’s dollar bonds traded near record lows Tuesday as a deepening political crisis in the West African nation turned a top emerging-markets performer into the worst.

item.title: How Lawsuits Alleging Social Media Harms Threaten Big Tech
item.description: A flood of lawsuits filed in recent years against social media giants claim that by engineering their platforms to be hard to resist, the companies are causing serious harm to young users. In the first case to go to trial, a jury in March found Meta Platforms Inc. and Google negligent in the design and operation of their platforms — a landmark verdict.

item.title: UniCredit Says Commerzbank Offer Set to Push Direct Stake to 34%
item.description: UniCredit SpA’s €38.6 billion ($45 billion) takeover offer for Commerzbank AG has been accepted by enough investors to push the stake over 30% once it takes ownership of the shares.

item.title: Russia Gushes Crude Exports as Drone Strikes Hobble Refineries
item.description: Year-to-date flows exceed averages for each year since Moscow’s troops invaded Ukraine in 2022

item.title: Barclays Pushes UK to Be More Welcoming to Foreign Investment
item.description: Barclays Plc urged the UK to do more to attract a broader range of foreign investment and help add trillions of pounds more capital from overseas.

item.title: Virgin Galactic’s 200% Rally Burns Shorts as Retail Rushes In
item.description: A rapid rally in shares of Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc. has burned short sellers betting against the space tourism firm, according to data from S3 Partners.

item.title: BofA Sees Strong South Africa Deal Market Despite Global Turmoil
item.description: Bank of America Corp. expects strong deal-making activity in South Africa to continue in this year despite ongoing global volatility, the head of its local operation said.

item.title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Model Access to 150 Additional Groups
item.description: Anthropic PBC is allowing 150 additional organizations around the world to access Mythos, an artificial intelligence model aimed at finding cybersecurity vulnerabilities that the company has said was too dangerous to make available to the general public.

item.title: AI Voice Startup ElevenLabs Targets Warsaw Hub Expansion in Push for CEE Clients
item.description: ElevenLabs sees Warsaw as its fastest-growing hub as the artificial intelligence voice company chases new customers in central and eastern Europe.

item.title: T-Mobile Wants to Sell a Lifestyle, Not Just Wireless
item.description: Felisha White, a 34-year-old T-Mobile US Inc. customer in New York, stays up late on Monday nights to see what the latest deal drops will be from her wireless provider.

item.title: Albania PM Vows to Press Ahead With Kushner Resort Amid Protests
item.description: Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama vowed to press ahead with a €4 billion ($4.7 billion) luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump, which has sparked mounting protests.

item.title: South Africa Beats Primary Budget Surplus Goal, Stabilizes Debt
item.description: South Africa posted a third consecutive primary budget surplus, underscoring the government’s commitment to repairing state finances.
